back: An Eulerian trail is a trail in a finite graph that visits every edge exactly once.
An Eulerian circuit is an Eulerian trail that starts and ends on the same vertex, forming a closed loop.
An Eulerian graph is a graph that contains an Eulerian circuit. A graph is Eulerian if and only if every vertex has an even degree and the graph is connected (ignoring isolated vertices).

front: What is the necessary and sufficient condition for a connected graph to have an Eulerian trail?
back: A connected graph has an Eulerian trail if and only if it has exactly zero or two vertices of odd degree. If there are zero odd-degree vertices, the trail is a circuit.
Euler's Theorem states: A connected graph G is Eulerian (has an Eulerian circuit) if and only if every vertex of G has an even degree.
